name: Semiconductors (SMH/SOX)
summary: Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **SMH $576.31 (+1.7% 1D, +31.9% 1M, +41.9% 3M) — AI INFRASTRUCTURE DEMAND CONTINUES TO EXCEED HELIUM-CONSTRAINT IMPACT; Kornbluth 'tsunami a thousand miles offshore' framework intact.** **KOSPI -1.7% 1D (first profit-taking session of cycle after extraordinary vertical run) but still +33.1% 1M / +39.3% 3M;** TAIEX 41,898.32 (+0.3% 1D, +18.3% 1M, +24.1% 3M); NIKKEI 62,742.57 (+0.5% 1D, +17.4% 1M, +8.9% 3M); NASDAQ 26,274.13 (+0.1% 1D, +14.7% 1M, +16.3% 3M). **B9 HMM Namu forensics public May 11 — Seoul stopped short of publicly blaming Iran**, creating diplomatic friction with Trump (who pre-attributed). Forensic finding: 2 unidentified aerial objects ~1 min apart at the stern caused engine-room fire; mine + torpedo RULED OUT; engine debris recovered. First commercial-vessel strike of cycle with conclusive public forensic investigation. Korea Times: strike 'may shift Seoul stance' on potential South Korean involvement in any Hormuz protection mission. Korea-Iran-attribution friction with Trump = USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 (+1.9% 1D). **Helium tail-risk timeline tightening: Korean fab buffer now ~4 WEEKS REMAINING to mid-June exhaustion** (TrendForce framework). Tier-1 chipmaker production-halt falsification still HOLDS but window contracting. **AI-CapEx demand from Microsoft/Meta/Amazon unbroken — secular driver continues to dominate.** Linde $504.40 +2.3% 1D (industrial-gas pricing power on persistent helium shortage). For India deployment thesis: AI flows continue to bypass India IT services (Nifty IT -3% May 12 intraday despite weak rupee tailwind = US-growth fears dominating) and rotate to Korea/Taiwan/US tech.

recent_signals.headline: MAY 8 TAPE CONFIRMS LARGEST 30-DAY MOVE IN ANY MAJOR INDEX IN RECENT MEMORY: SMH $566.54 (+4.9% 1D, +31.7% 1M, +39.3% 3M FRESH PEAK); KOSPI 7,498.00 (+0.1% 1D, +36.5% 1M, +41.5% 3M); SK HYNIX +144% YTD; SAMSUNG +111% YTD; MICRON, SANDISK +15% EACH MAY 8 ON AI DATA-CENTER BUILDOUT DEMAND; NVIDIA +2%; MSCI EM +2.0% 1D PULLED BY KOREA WEIGHT (~14% OF EEM). With Brent at $101 and Hormuz blocked Day 71, the AI cycle is producing the largest 30-day rally in major-market history. Markets making binary call: AI capex super-cycle DOMINATES Iran-war risk premium. **Helium tail-risk pricing-through (~5% crisis discount only)** — Cheniere CCO publicly: 'astounded that gas prices haven't risen more.' Korean fab buffer dates 6-12 weeks per Smith/Discovery Alert puts absorptive capacity through approximately mid-June. **If Hormuz remains closed past Korean fab buffers depleting, the KOSPI 36.5% gain is THE most reversible position in the world.** Reversibility: ~5% crisis-discount reverses on ceasefire; ~95% structural AI capex story persists. Apple-Samsung-Intel chip-production talks remain preliminary, no orders placed.
sources: Smith / Discovery Alert (Korean fab helium buffers)
